info.title: ServerBee API
info.description: ServerBee VPS monitoring probe API. All responses are wrapped in `{"data": <value>}`. Errors return `{"error": {"code": "...", "message": "..."}}`.

200.description: Current per-IP rate limit state across login, register, and public status buckets
Agent registered against the bound server

401.description: Invalid, expired, revoked, or already-used enrollment code

400.description: Server is pending (no token to rotate); use recover instead

404.description: Alert state or rule not found

200.description: Number of audit log entries removed
Filter options for the audit log

get.summary: Redirect user to the OAuth provider's authorization page.
Handle the OAuth callback from the provider.

200.description: Default dashboard with widgets (auto-creates if none exists)

400.description: Validation error (e.g. cannot unset default, unknown widget type)
Cannot delete default or last dashboard

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: RFC3339 timestamp from a previous `next_cursor` response.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by status (investigating, identified, monitoring, resolved).

200.description: IP quality status-change events for a server
IP quality overview for all servers
IP quality data for a server

409.description: CAP_IP_QUALITY is not effective for this server

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor from `next_cursor` of the previous response.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Cap on returned buckets. Defaults to 50, max 500.

post.summary:
Create a pending server row and a server-bound enrollment in a single
transaction. The server row is inserted with `token_hash = NULL` (pending),
`capabilities = CAP_DEFAULT`, and `protocol_version = 1`. The operator-
supplied `tags` are persisted in `server_tags`, and the global default
network probe targets are applied to the new server. The returned plaintext
enrollment `code` is shown exactly once — the install command on the agent
will consume it via `POST /api/agent/register`.
post.description:
`caps` is accepted in the request for the install.sh `--caps` arg but is
NOT persisted on the server row. The server row always starts at
`CAP_DEFAULT`; the operator can edit capabilities afterwards.

200.description: Server created (pending) and bound enrollment minted

400.description: Validation error or max_servers cap reached

post.summary:
Mint a fresh bound enrollment for an already-enrolled server so the operator
can reinstall the agent. The target server MUST already have a token
(`token_hash IS NOT NULL`) — recover on a pending server is rejected with
`400`, use `regenerate-code` for that path.
post.description:
Recover NEVER auto-supersedes an outstanding enrollment: if one is still
active, this returns `409` and the operator is expected to either wait for
it to expire or revoke it first. Only `regenerate-code` auto-supersedes.

`revoke_immediately`:
- `true` — clear `token_hash`/`token_prefix` inside the same transaction
  and kick the currently connected agent WS after commit. The server
  returns to pending until the new code is consumed.
- `false` — the existing token stays valid; the new code only becomes
  active once the agent registers with it (`verify_and_consume_tx` then
  rotates the token via `mint_token_for_server`).

409.description: Outstanding enrollment exists; revoke it first

post.summary:
Mint a fresh bound enrollment for a pending server, auto-superseding the
previous outstanding enrollment (if any) inside one transaction. The target
server MUST be pending (`token_hash IS NULL`); use `recover` for an already-
enrolled server.
post.description:
Optimistic concurrency: callers pass `expected_enrollment_id` to guard
against stomping on a concurrent operator's regenerated code. Semantics:
- `Some(id) && matches current outstanding` → proceed (CAS pass)
- `Some(id) && does NOT match` (including: there is no outstanding row, or
  the row referenced has been revoked/consumed) → 409
- `None && outstanding exists` → proceed (last-writer-wins)
- `None && no outstanding` → proceed (fresh mint)

400.description: Server is not pending; use recover instead

404.description: Server not found or not online

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: Number of days to include (default: 90, min: 1, max: 365).

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter by monitor type (ssl, dns, http_keyword, tcp, whois).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return.

422.description: Validation error or custom themes disabled

post.summary: Download a backup of the SQLite database.

Restore the database from an uploaded backup file.
The server should be restarted after restore.
Note: request body is raw binary (application/octet-stream).

Validation error or custom themes disabled

Validation error or custom themes disabled

Public status page or IP-quality panel disabled
Public status page or maintenance panel disabled

Public status page or network panel disabled

Public status page or server-detail panel disabled

200.description: Traffic overview for all servers with billing cycles
Global daily traffic aggregation across all servers

parameters.name: history
parameters.description: Number of historical billing cycles to return (default: 6).

200.description: Current cycle and historical cycle traffic

event_at.description: first_triggered_at for firing, resolved_at for resolved

oneOf.description:
Auto-block the `source_ip` from the triggering security event.
Only valid on `ssh_brute_force_detected` / `port_scan_detected` rules.

AlertRuleAction.description:
Side-effect action attached to an alert rule. Currently only
`block_source_ip` is supported, which is restricted to security rules whose
payload carries a `source_ip` (`ssh_brute_force_detected` /
`port_scan_detected`).

oneOf.description:
Parameters for security-typed rules (`ssh_brute_force_detected`,
`ssh_new_ip_login`, `port_scan_detected`). Ignored for metric rules.

items.description: A single day's uptime data, with gap-filling for missing dates.

target.description:
Bare IP or CIDR. Server canonicalizes to a CIDR (`/32` or `/128` for
bare IPs, network-bits stripped for CIDRs).

rules.description: JSON array of ordered match rules

is_public.description: Whether this incident is shown on the public status page.
Whether this maintenance window is shown on the public status page.

caps.description:
Capabilities to encode into the install.sh `--caps` arg only; not
persisted on the server row (which always uses `CAP_DEFAULT`).

code.description:
Plaintext enrollment code — shown exactly once at mint time. The UI
must surface this to the operator and warn that it cannot be recovered.

MobileTokenResponse.description: Token pair returned after successful login or refresh.

MobileUserResponse.description: Minimal user info returned alongside tokens.

OutstandingEnrollmentSummary.description:
Outstanding-enrollment summary returned alongside a `ServerResponse` so the
UI can render pending state and offer the install command without a second
fetch. The plaintext code is only ever returned by the mint endpoints —
never here.

PublicIncidentsResponse.description:
Payload shape for `/api/status/incidents`. Splits active vs recent
(resolved-within-7-days) incidents so the SPA can render them in separate
regions without a second query.

oneOf.description: Absent until the agent has reported at least one snapshot.

region.description:
Service-specific unlock region (e.g. "US-NY" for Netflix); distinct
from the egress IP's geographic region, which is stripped.

device_token.description: The APNs device token obtained from the iOS device.

blocked.description: True if `count >= max` and the window is still open.

max.description: Configured maximum requests per window for this scope.

seconds_remaining.description: Seconds until the window resets. Zero if already expired.

window_seconds.description:
Window length in seconds. Login/register share a 15-minute window
while the public bucket uses 60 seconds; surface it per-entry so
callers don't have to special-case scopes.

window_start.description: RFC 3339 timestamp the current window opened.

auth_window_seconds.description: Window length (seconds) for the login + register buckets.

public_window_seconds.description: Window length (seconds) for the public-status bucket.

ip.description: Optional IP filter; when omitted, clears every entry in the selected scope(s).

oneOf.description: Optional scope filter; when omitted, clears every bucket.

RecordedProtocol.description:
Persisted/read protocol enum. Extends `TraceProtocol` with `Legacy` for
records normalized from pre-trippy agents whose actual probe mode is
unknown (Unix `traceroute` defaults to UDP, `mtr` is ICMP, Windows
`tracert` is ICMP — the legacy agent does not report which ran).

revoke_immediately.description:
If `true`, clear the server's `token_hash`/`token_prefix` and kick the
currently connected agent WebSocket as part of the same transaction.
Use this when the operator suspects the existing agent token has been
compromised. If `false`, the existing token remains valid and only a
new bound enrollment is minted alongside it.

expected_enrollment_id.description:
Optimistic concurrency token. If `Some`, must match the current
outstanding enrollment id exactly; otherwise the server returns 409.
If `None`, last-writer-wins: any outstanding enrollment is revoked
and a fresh one is minted.

token.description:
New plaintext run token — shown once. The agent must be reconfigured
with this value (or it will need to re-enroll).

next_cursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page. `None` when there are no more rows.

dedupe_window_seconds.description: Notification dedupe window per (rule, server, source_ip).

min_distinct_ports.description: Minimum distinct ports scanned to fire (`port_scan_detected`).

ServerIpQualityData.description:
IP quality data for a single server: its unlock results and the latest
IP-quality snapshot (if any). Used by both the per-server summary endpoint
and the all-servers overview endpoint.

ServerResponse.description: Server response DTO — excludes sensitive fields (token_hash, token_prefix).

has_token.description:
`true` iff the server row has a non-NULL `token_hash`. Pending servers
(created via `POST /api/servers` but not yet enrolled by an agent) have
`has_token = false`; the UI uses this to render a "pending" badge.

oneOf.description:
The single outstanding (not consumed, not revoked) bound enrollment for
this server, if any. Plaintext code is intentionally NOT included — it
is only returned at mint time. The UI uses this to surface a "show
install command" button on pending or recovering servers.

ThemeReferences.description:
Where a custom theme is referenced.

After R1 the public status page no longer carries its own `theme_ref` —
only the admin UI's active theme can pin a custom theme. The previous
`status_pages: Vec<StatusPageRef>` collection has therefore been removed.

TraceProtocol.description:
Strict input protocol enum used on `ServerMessage::Traceroute.protocol`
and on the server's POST request DTO. Only the three values the user can
pick are accepted; legacy is NOT part of this enum.

ips.description: All IPs that responded for this TTL (ECMP). Empty when no response yet.

stddev_ms.description: RTT standard deviation across all received probes.

TransferInfo.description: Serializable transfer info for API responses.

UpdateStatusPage.description:
Partial-update payload for the singleton status_page row.

Every field is optional so the admin UI can PATCH individual toggles
without re-sending the entire config. Matches the prevailing admin
update-DTO convention in this codebase (see `UpdateMaintenance`,
`UpdateIncident`, `UpdateServerInput`): `Option<T>` = leave alone,
`Option<Option<T>>` = leave alone / clear / set for nullable columns.

description.description:
Nullable in the entity — `Option<Option<String>>` so callers can
distinguish "leave alone" (absent / null at the outer Option) from
"explicitly clear" (`Some(None)`). Mirrors `UpdateMaintenance`.

server_ids.description:
Replace the full set of pinned servers. `None` = leave alone,
`Some(vec![])` = explicitly no servers. The service serialises
this to the entity's `server_ids_json` storage column.

UptimeDailyEntry.description: A single day's uptime data, with gap-filling for missing dates.

tags.name: rate-limit
tags.description: In-memory per-IP rate limit inspection & manual reset (admin only)

tags.name: cost
tags.description: VPS cost insights and value scoring

tags.name: security
tags.description: Security events (SSH brute force, port scans, new IP logins)

tags.name: ip-quality
tags.description: IP quality: service unlock detection + IP metadata & risk scoring

tags.name: geoip
tags.description: GeoIP MMDB database management (DB-IP Lite Country)

tags.name: asn
tags.description: ASN MMDB database management for traceroute enrichment (DB-IP Lite ASN)
